name: Head in the Clouds
image_prompt: Interior of Head in the Clouds, the airborne pleasure-dirigible from Richard Morgan's Altered Carbon, hovering above near-future Bay City at dusk. A wide circular salon inside the upper deck of a colossal lighter-than-air airship, opulent and decadent. Plush dark-purple velvet booths and low brass tables in a circular arrangement around a sunken central lounge floor. A polished black marble floor inset with thin glowing fiber-optic veins of magenta. A large curved panoramic window wall along the outer hull showing the cloud sea below, the silhouette of a near-future Bay City skyline lit in cyan and pink neon receding to the horizon, the orbital tether faintly visible like a thin vertical line going up into the sky. Recessed magenta and amber lighting in the curved ceiling. A small chrome-and-glass bar on one side stocked with backlit bottles. Holographic koi swimming in mid-air near the ceiling. Empty, no people, no figures. Colors: deep purple velvet, polished black marble, magenta glow, amber, neon pink and cyan city lights, twilight blue sky. Decadent neo-noir Altered-Carbon cyberpunk atmosphere.
location: The upper salon of Head in the Clouds, a converted military lighter-than-air dirigible repurposed into a high-end orbiting pleasure-house, currently station-keeping six kilometers above Bay City. The owner is offworld. The salon has been closed to clientele for the night. The working group has the run of the upper deck. The lower decks are private and remain so.
time: Dusk. The horizon below the airship is a thin gold band fading to deep blue. Down at street level it is already night.
weather: The dirigible's hull holds the salon at 21 degrees and faint perfume. Outside the panoramic window, the upper-atmosphere wind has the airship at a slight three-degree list. Below the cloud floor, weather is happening to other people.

atmosphere.lighting: Recessed magenta in the ceiling, amber accent in the booths, fiber-optic veins of pink in the black marble floor pulsing on a slow circulatory rhythm. The curved window wall throws the city's neon up into the salon in cool cyan and pink across every glass surface. The bar back-light is cold white and surgical and feels deliberately out of step with the rest of the room.
atmosphere.sound: A low ambient set on the salon's audio system, jazz played at the speed of memory. The faint creak of the airship's frame in the wind. The whisper of climate control. A door closing somewhere on a deck below. The almost-not-there bass thump of something heavier playing on a deck the working group is not invited to.
atmosphere.crowd: Empty of clients. The booths feel populated by their absence. The bartender has been sent off-deck. The salon's resident AI is in a mode the staff calls 'discreet,' which means it is watching but will not interrupt. A thin man in a dark suit stands at the far end of the bar reading a paperback. He is not staff and he has not introduced himself.
atmosphere.smell: Expensive jasmine, the faint chemical sweetness of the airship's lift gas leaking through somewhere it should not, polished leather, gun oil from the man at the bar, a single lit clove cigarette in an ashtray on a booth that nobody currently occupies

layout.central_lounge: A sunken circular floor of black marble, surrounded by a ring of plush deep-purple velvet booths. The fiber-optic veins in the marble pulse pink in slow waves outward from the center. The center holds a low brass table currently empty except for a half-finished glass of single-malt that nobody at the booths has claimed.
layout.panorama_window: A curving floor-to-ceiling window along the entire outer hull of the salon, looking out at the slow-moving sea of clouds below and the receding cyan-and-pink lights of Bay City. The orbital tether is faintly visible as a vertical line going up into the dusk. Touching the glass at any point dims the recessed lighting nearest you for privacy.
layout.chrome_bar: A long bar in chrome and smoked glass against one curved wall, backlit shelves stocked with offworld liquors, a polished espresso unit, a pair of crystal decanters, and a row of clean glasses lined up as if for a ceremony. The bartender's apron is folded on the counter. He went somewhere quietly.
layout.private_alcoves: Three velvet-curtained alcoves recessed into the inner wall of the salon, each fitted with a low couch and a small table. The curtains are currently drawn open. The middle alcove has a paperback novel placed face down on the cushion. The novel is in a script nobody in the working group reads.
layout.transit_corridor: An archway leading to the staff corridor and the spiral stair down to the lower decks. The corridor is dim, lit only by green standby strips along the floor. The stair descends into the parts of the airship the working group has been politely instructed not to visit.

objects_of_note: A koi-school of pale-pink holographic fish drifting through the air near the salon's ceiling, slow and silent. Touching one with a finger makes it scatter into a brief cloud of pixels and re-form a meter away.
An ashtray with a single lit clove cigarette burning in an unoccupied booth. Smoke rising in a perfectly straight column because the salon's ventilation has been deliberately stilled.
A black plastic key card on the chrome bar, blank, no markings, magnetic strip oriented up. Nobody put it there in the last twenty minutes.
A paperback in the middle alcove, face-down on the cushion. The cover script reads vertically and is unfamiliar. A cocktail napkin marks a page near the back.

The thin man at the bar, reading a paperback in a different unfamiliar script. He has not turned a page in nine minutes. His glass of mineral water has not been touched. His suit is well cut. He is not openly armed.
mood: Decadent, surveilled, and provisional. Head in the Clouds is the kind of room where every soft surface is a recording device and every hard surface is a contract. The view through the panorama window is the most expensive thing the salon offers, and it is meant to remind anyone in the booths that they are above the world's jurisdiction in every direction that matters. Frameworks brought in here have to reckon with the fact that the airship is owned by people who are not currently aboard and whose discretion is a service they bill for. Decisions made here are made very softly. The bartender always returns. The thin man always finishes his book.
